props_visible: Clear tea glass (traditional small glass, Arab tea style)
Traditional woven kilim cushion/fabric in background (red-green-cream striped)

characters_visible.wardrobe_notes: White thobe, red-and-white ghutrah, black iqal — full traditional Saudi dress
characters_visible.gesture_notes: Right hand holds brass teapot and pours; left hand holds/supports glass receiving the pour — standard hospitable two-hand tea service

notable_visual_elements: Tea pour action captured mid-stream — dynamic hospitality moment
Right hand serving (pouring) = culturally correct Saudi hospitality etiquette
Left hand holds the guest's cup — reverent 'I hold it for you' gesture
Genuine warm smile creates authentic connection moment
Gold brass teapot modernizes traditional service — same ritual, premium vessel

hospitality_cues: Tea pouring with right hand = correct Islamic/Saudi service etiquette
Holding guest's cup with left while pouring with right = the highest hospitality gesture (you serve the guest's cup)
The smile during service = Arab hospitality warmth (khidma min al-qalb — service from the heart)
cultural_notes.free_notes: This single image communicates the entire @aseeb.najd brand philosophy. The right-hand pour + left-hand cup-hold is the most specific Saudi hospitality etiquette detail possible. The gold teapot is premium without being flashy. The smile is genuine. The traditional dress grounds it in Najdi identity. If you were to pick one image to explain why this account scores Saudi Score 9, this is it.

pattern_matches.notes: Brass teapot + pour action = condensed Saudi hospitality ritual in one frame
Tea service in traditional dress is the quintessential Najdi hospitality visual story
Tea stream captures texture/action mid-pour — liquid motion as visual anchor
